cmd.up.long:
Start all services and dependencies for the current project.

Raioz auto-detects the config file (raioz.yaml, .raioz.json) and
the runtime of each service (Docker Compose, Dockerfile, npm, Go, Make, etc.).

Services start in dependency order. Infrastructure (images) starts first,
then application services with their native tools.
cmd.down.long:
Bring down all services and infrastructure for the current project.

Use --all to stop all workspace services and infrastructure.
Use --prune-shared to also stop shared infrastructure if no other active projects use it.
cmd.status.long:
Show detailed status information for all services including:
- Status (running/stopped)
- Health status (healthy/unhealthy/starting)
- Uptime
- Resource usage (CPU, Memory)
- Version/commit information
- Last update time
cmd.logs.long:
View logs for one or more services.

If no service is specified and --all is not set, shows logs for all services.
Use --all to explicitly show logs for all services.
Use --follow to follow logs in real-time.
Use --tail N to show only the last N lines.
cmd.clean.short: Clean up stopped services and unused resources
cmd.clean.long:
Clean up stopped services and unused Docker resources.

By default, cleans stopped services for the current project.
Use flags to clean additional resources or all projects.
cmd.check.short: Check for alignment issues between config and state
cmd.check.long:
Check if the current configuration aligns with the saved state.

Detects:
- Configuration changes (branches, tags, ports, dependencies)
- Branch drift (manual branch changes in repositories)
- Image tag changes
- Other misalignments

Exit codes:
- 0: All checks passed or only info issues (branch drift)
- 1: Critical or warning issues found
cmd.ci.long:
Optimized command for CI/CD pipelines with:
- Fast validations
- JSON output format
- Ephemeral environments support
- Automatic cleanup

This command is designed to be used in CI/CD pipelines where speed and
parseable output are critical.
cmd.compare.long:
Compare your local .raioz.json with a production Docker Compose file
to identify differences in images, ports, volumes, and dependencies.

This helps ensure that your local development environment matches production
as closely as possible.
cmd.health.short: Check project health and manage local project lifecycle
cmd.health.long:
Check if the project is running and report its health status.

Uses the health endpoint defined in raioz.yaml (e.g., health: /api/health)
or falls back to checking if containers and processes are alive.
cmd.ignore.long:
Manage services that should be ignored during dependency resolution.

Ignored services will not be cloned, built, or started. However, if other
services depend on ignored services, a warning will be shown.

Example:
  raioz ignore add service-name
  raioz ignore remove service-name
  raioz ignore list
cmd.ignore.add.short: Add a service to the ignore list
cmd.ignore.add.long:
Add a service to the ignore list. The service will not be cloned,
built, or started during raioz up.

Example:
  raioz ignore add old-service
cmd.ignore.remove.short: Remove a service from the ignore list
cmd.ignore.remove.long:
Remove a service from the ignore list. The service will be processed
normally on the next raioz up.

Example:
  raioz ignore remove service-name
cmd.ignore.list.long:
List all services that are currently in the ignore list.

Example:
  raioz ignore list
cmd.init.short: Initialize a new raioz.yaml configuration file
cmd.init.long:
Scan the current directory and generate a raioz.yaml configuration file.

Detects services from subdirectory structure and infers infrastructure
dependencies from .env files (DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, etc.).
cmd.link.short: Manage service symlinks for external editing
cmd.link.long:
Manage symlinks from Raioz workspace to external paths.

This allows you to edit service code in an external location while Raioz
manages the service from its workspace directory.

Example:
  raioz link payments ~/dev/payments
  raioz unlink payments
cmd.link.add.short: Create a symlink from workspace to external path
cmd.link.add.long:
Create a symlink from the Raioz workspace service directory to an
external path. This allows you to edit the service code in the external
location while Raioz uses it from the workspace.

The external path must exist and be a directory. If the service path already
exists as a directory (not a symlink), the command will fail.

Example:
  raioz link add payments ~/dev/payments
cmd.link.remove.long:
Remove a symlink from a service. This will remove the symlink but
will not delete the service from the workspace or the external directory.

After removing the symlink, the service will need to be cloned again if it
was originally a Git repository.

Example:
  raioz link remove payments
  raioz link unlink payments
cmd.link.list.long:
List all services that are currently linked to external paths.

Example:
  raioz link list
cmd.list.long:
List all active projects tracked in the global state.

This command shows:
- Project names
- Last execution time
- Number of active services per project
- Workspace paths

Filters:
- Use --filter to show only projects matching a name pattern
- Use --status to filter by service status (running, stopped)
cmd.migrate.long:
Convert a production Docker Compose file to .raioz.json format.

This command helps you bootstrap a local .raioz.json from an existing
production docker-compose.yml file. You may need to manually adjust
the generated configuration for your local development needs.
cmd.override.short: Override a service with a local path
cmd.override.long:
Override a service to use a local path instead of the Git repository or image
defined in .raioz.json.

This command does NOT modify .raioz.json. Instead, it registers an override
in ~/.raioz/overrides.json that takes precedence over .raioz.json.

The override will be automatically reverted if the path no longer exists.

Example:
  raioz override orders --path ~/dev/orders
  raioz override api --path /opt/custom/api
cmd.override.list.long: List all registered service overrides from ~/.raioz/overrides.json.
cmd.override.remove.long:
Remove a service override. The service will fall back to the configuration
in .raioz.json.
cmd.ports.short: List all ports in use by active projects
cmd.version.long:
Show version information for raioz including:
- Binary version
- Git commit (if available)
- Build date
- Supported schema version
cmd.volumes.long:
Manage Docker volumes associated with a project.

Subcommands:
  list    List all volumes for the project
  remove  Remove specific or all project volumes

Examples:
  raioz volumes list
  raioz volumes remove --all
  raioz volumes remove my-project_postgres-data
cmd.volumes.list.long:
List all Docker volumes associated with the project.

Shows volume names, their source (service or infra), and whether
they are shared with other projects.

Examples:
  raioz volumes list
  raioz volumes list --project myproject
cmd.volumes.remove.long:
Remove Docker volumes associated with the project.

You can remove specific volumes by name or all volumes with --all.
Volumes shared with other projects are excluded unless forced.

Examples:
  raioz volumes remove --all
  raioz volumes remove --all --force
  raioz volumes remove my-project_postgres-data
cmd.workspace.long:
Manage workspaces for organizing multiple projects.

A workspace allows you to organize multiple projects and switch between them.
The active workspace is stored in ~/.raioz/active-workspace and can be used
as a default when working with projects.
cmd.workspace.use.long:
Set the active workspace to use for future commands.

If the workspace does not exist, it will be created automatically.
The active workspace will be loaded automatically when using raioz commands
if no project is explicitly specified.

Example:
  raioz workspace use empresa-x
  raioz workspace use billing-platform
cmd.workspace.list.long:
List all available workspaces and show which one is currently active.

Example:
  raioz workspace list
cmd.workspace.delete.long:
Delete a workspace and all its contents (state, compose files, env).

This does NOT delete cloned service repositories.
If the deleted workspace is the active one, it will be deactivated.

Example:
  raioz workspace delete old-project
flag.up.file: Path to config file (default: auto-detect raioz.yaml)
flag.force_reclone: Force re-clone of all git repositories
flag.dry_run: Show what would be done without making changes
flag.up.dry_run: Show what would be done without making changes (dependency assist only)
flag.up.only: Start only these services and their dependencies
flag.tail: Number of lines to show from the end of logs (0 = all)
flag.down.all: Stop all workspace services and infra (full shutdown)
flag.prune_shared: Also stop infra if no other active projects use it
flag.log_level: Set log level (debug, info, warn, error)
flag.ci.keep: Keep ephemeral environment after CI run (for debugging)
flag.ci.job_id: CI job ID for ephemeral environment naming
flag.ci.skip_build: Skip building and starting services (validation only)
flag.ci.only_validate: Only run validations, skip all setup
flag.list.filter: Filter projects by name (partial match, case-insensitive)
flag.list.status: Filter projects by service status (running, stopped)
flag.override.path: Local path to override the service (required)

output.no_state_found: No state file found. Nothing to stop (workspace already stopped).
output.no_conflicting_projects: No active projects collide with this project's host ports.

output.link_external_not_deleted: The external directory was not deleted
output.workspace_create_hint: Create a workspace by running: raioz workspace use <name>
output.workspace_set_hint: Set an active workspace by running: raioz workspace use <name>
output.workspace_deactivated: Deactivated workspace '%s' (it was the active workspace)
output.workspace_delete_confirm: Delete workspace '%s' and all its contents? (y/N):
error.workspace_check_exists: failed to check if workspace exists
cmd.restart.long:
Restart one or more services for the current project.

Specify service names to restart specific services.
Use --all to restart all services (excluding infra by default).
Use --include-infra with --all to also restart infrastructure.
Use --force-recreate to tear down and recreate containers.
flag.restart.all: Restart all services (excluding infra by default)
flag.restart.force_recreate: Force recreate containers instead of simple restart
error.restart_suggestion: Check if services are running with 'raioz status'. Try 'raioz down' and 'raioz up' if restart fails.
error.restart_no_services: No services specified. Use service names as arguments or --all flag.
error.restart_not_running: Project is not running. Use 'raioz up' to start it first.
error.restart_service_not_found: Service '%s' not found in the compose file
error.restart_host_service: Service '%s' runs on the host (source.command) and cannot be restarted with this command. Stop and restart the project with 'raioz down && raioz up'.
cmd.exec.short: Run a command inside a running service container
cmd.exec.long:
Run a command inside a running service container.

If no command is specified, opens a shell (sh).

Examples:
  raioz exec api sh
  raioz exec postgres psql -U postgres
  raioz exec redis redis-cli
flag.exec.interactive: Keep stdin open and allocate TTY (default: true)
error.exec_not_running: Project is not running. Use 'raioz up' to start it first.
error.exec_service_not_found: Service '%s' not found in the compose file
error.exec_host_service: Service '%s' runs on the host (source.command), not in a Docker container. Use 'raioz logs %s' to see its output instead.
error.ignore_add: failed to add to ignore list
error.ignore_remove: failed to remove from ignore list
output.override_service_not_found: Service '%s' not found in current .raioz.json. Override will still be registered.
output.override_already_exists: Service '%s' already has an override pointing to: %s
output.override_run_up_project: Run 'raioz up' in project '%s' to apply the override.
output.override_run_up: Run 'raioz up' to apply the override.
output.override_run_up_apply: Run 'raioz up' to apply the change.
app.missing_deps_dry_run: Dry-run mode: dependencies shown but not added
app.missing_deps_cannot_add: Cannot add dependency '%s': no definition found
app.missing_deps_stub_not_impl: Stub mode not implemented yet, ignoring dependency '%s'
app.missing_deps_ignored_warn: Services may fail if these dependencies are required
app.conflict_dry_run: Dry-run mode: conflicts shown but not resolved
error.no_project: Could not determine project name. Please provide --file or --project flag.
error.workspace_resolve_suggestion: Check that the project name is correct. Verify workspace directories exist and are accessible.
error.not_running: Project is not running (no state file found)
error.state_load_suggestion: The state file may be corrupted. You can try removing the state file manually.
error.lock_failed: Failed to acquire lock (another raioz process may be running)
error.lock_suggestion: Wait for the other process to finish, or remove the lock file manually if the process crashed.
error.docker_down_failed: Failed to stop Docker Compose services
error.docker_down_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status with 'docker ps'. Verify that Docker Compose is installed and working. Services may already be stopped.
error.state_remove_suggestion: Check file permissions. You can try removing the state file manually.
error.preflight_suggestion: Ensure Docker is installed and running, Git is installed, and you have sufficient disk space.
error.no_project_suggestion: Please provide --file or --project flag to specify the project.
error.config_load_suggestion: Ensure .raioz.json exists and is valid JSON. Use --config flag to specify a different path if needed.
error.state_load_suggestion_recreate: This may indicate a corrupted state file. Try running 'raioz down' and then 'raioz up' again to recreate the state.
error.local_check: Failed to check if project is local
error.volumes_no_target: Specify volume names to remove or use --all to remove all project volumes
error.volume_not_found: Volume '%s' not found in this project
error.volumes_extract_services: Failed to extract named volumes from services
error.volumes_extract_infra: Failed to extract named volumes from infra
error.production_path_required_suggestio: Please specify the path to the production docker-compose.yml file using --production flag
error.config_load_local: Failed to load local config from %s
error.config_load_production: Failed to load production config from %s
error.compare_critical: Critical differences found between local and production configurations
error.compare_critical_suggestion: Review the differences above and update your .raioz.json to match production
error.logs_not_running: Project is not running (no state file found)
error.project_env_resolve_suggestion: Check that you have write permissions for the env directory.
error.global_env_write: Failed to write global environment variables
error.global_env_write_suggestion: Check that you have write permissions for the env directory.
error.config_load_from_suggestion: Ensure .raioz.json exists and is valid JSON. Use --config flag to specify a different path.
error.workspace_resolve_permissions_sugg: Ensure you have write permissions for workspace directories. Check README.md for workspace locations.
error.preflight_suggestion_detail: Ensure Docker is installed and running, Git is installed, and you have sufficient disk space. Check the error details above for specific issues.
error.proxy_mkcert_missing: proxy is enabled with tls: mkcert, but mkcert was not found on PATH and no pre-generated certificates exist in ~/.raioz/certs/. Raioz would silently fall back to HTTP, which breaks services that enforce HTTPS (Keycloak, OIDC, HSTS).
error.proxy_mkcert_missing_suggestion:
Install mkcert from https://github.com/FiloSottile/mkcert and run `mkcert -install`, then re-run `raioz up`.
  Alternatively set `proxy.tls: letsencrypt` for a real public domain, or remove `proxy:` from raioz.yaml to skip HTTPS entirely.
error.dependency_conflicts_suggestion: Check your configuration for conflicting service definitions. Resolve conflicts manually or use dependency assist.
error.dependency_conflicts_aborted_sugge: Resolve the conflicts shown above and try again.
error.dependency_assist_suggestion: Check that dependency definitions are accessible. Verify network connectivity and repository access.
error.dependency_assist_aborted: Aborted by user during dependency assist
error.dependency_assist_aborted_suggesti: Resolve missing dependencies and try again.
error.port_validate_suggestion: Check that port validation can access workspace directories. Verify file permissions and workspace structure.
error.port_conflicts_suggestion: Resolve port conflicts by changing port mappings in your configuration. Each service must use unique ports.
error.port_conflict_explicit: %s and %s both declare port %d. They cannot share the same host port.
error.port_conflict_explicit_suggestion: Change one service to a different port, or remove the explicit `port:` from one so raioz can auto-assign it.
error.port_conflict_host_explicit: Service '%s' declared port %d but it is already in use on the host.
error.port_conflict_host_explicit_sugges: Free the port (check `raioz ports` and any other dev tool bound to it) or change the service's declared port in raioz.yaml.
error.port_conflict_host_implicit: Service '%s' needs port %d but it is already in use on the host.
error.port_conflict_host_implicit_sugges: Close whatever is using the port, or declare an explicit `port:` in raioz.yaml to pin a different one.
error.port_allocation_exhausted: Could not find a free host port for service '%s'.
error.dep_publish_conflict_host_explicit: Dependency '%s' declared publish port %d but it is already in use on the host.
Free the port (check `raioz ports` and other dev tools bound to it) or change the dependency's `publish:` value in raioz.yaml.
error.dep_publish_conflict_host_auto: Dependency '%s' needs to publish port %d but it is already in use on the host.
error.dep_publish_conflict_host_auto_sug: Close whatever is using the port, or pin a specific `publish: <number>` in raioz.yaml so raioz can pick a different one.
warn.deps_ports_deprecated: dependency '%s' uses legacy `ports:`; consider migrating to `publish:` and `expose:` for clearer semantics
error.image_pull_failed: Failed to validate or pull Docker images
error.image_pull_suggestion: Check network connectivity and Docker daemon status. Verify that image names and tags are correct. Ensure you have permission to pull images. Try running 'docker pull <image>:<tag>' manually to test.
error.network_ensure_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status and permissions. Ensure you have permission to create Docker networks. Try running 'docker network ls' to verify Docker is working.
error.volume_extract_infra: Failed to extract named volumes from infra
error.volume_extract_services: Failed to extract named volumes from services
error.volume_ensure_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status and permissions. Ensure you have permission to create Docker volumes. Try running 'docker volume ls' to verify Docker is working.
error.compose_generate_failed: Failed to generate Docker Compose configuration
error.compose_generate_suggestion: Check your service configuration for errors. Verify that all required fields are present and valid. Review the error details above for specific issues.
error.infra_start_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status with 'docker ps'. Verify that Docker Compose is installed and working. Check logs with 'docker compose logs' for more details.
error.services_start_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status with 'docker ps'. Verify that Docker Compose is installed and working. Check logs with 'docker compose logs' for more details.
error.git_update_branch_failed: Failed to update repositories after branch changes
error.git_update_branch_suggestion: Check network connectivity and repository access. Verify that branch names are correct and accessible.
error.state_load_previous_suggestion: The state file may be corrupted. You can try removing it and running 'raioz up' again.
error.state_save_suggestion: Check that you have write permissions for the workspace directory.
error.feature_flag_validation_failed: Feature flag or mock configuration validation failed
error.feature_flag_validation_suggestion: Check your configuration for feature flag and mock configuration errors.
error.no_services_for_profile: No services or infra found for profile '%s'
error.no_services_for_profile_suggestion: Check that services and/or infra have the profile assigned in your configuration.
error.no_services_for_default_profiles: No services or infra found for default profiles %v
error.no_services_for_default_profiles_s: Check that services and/or infra have these profiles in your configuration.
error.ignored_services_load_failed: Failed to load ignored services list
error.ignored_services_load_suggestion: Check that ~/.raioz/ignore.json is valid JSON. You can try removing it and running 'raioz up' again.
error.ignored_services_filter_suggestion: Check your configuration for errors. Verify that service names in ignore list match your configuration.
error.no_services_or_commands: No services, infrastructure, or project commands configured
error.no_services_or_commands_suggestion: Configure at least one of the following: services, infrastructure, or 'project.commands.up'. If you have services but they're all filtered out, check feature flag configurations, environment variables, and ignore list.
error.operation_cancelled_suggestion: The workspace project remains running. To start the local project, first stop the workspace project with 'raioz down'.
error.override_apply_suggestion: Check ~/.raioz/overrides.json for invalid entries. Run 'raioz override list' to review.

up.env_files_error: Some .env files could not be generated from templates
up.waiting_healthy_before_cmd: Waiting for services to be healthy before executing project command...
up.services_not_healthy_warning: Some services may not be healthy yet, proceeding with project command anyway
up.compose_generate_error: Failed to generate Docker Compose configuration
up.waiting_infra_healthy: Waiting for infrastructure to be healthy...
up.infra_not_healthy_warning: Some infrastructure services may not be fully healthy yet, proceeding with services deployment anyway
up.images_verify_error: Failed to verify or pull Docker images
up.images_verified: All Docker images verified and ready
up.all_services_running: All services are already running (no changes detected)

up.git.ensure_error: Failed to ensure repository for service '%s'

error.only_service_not_found: Service or infra '%s' not found in configuration
up.conflict.would_start_from: This run would also start the same service from:
up.conflict.choose_resolution: Choose how to resolve (only one source can run this service):
up.conflict.opt_use_local: Use my local project — stop workspace service and run from my code (good for dev)
up.conflict.opt_use_workspace: Use workspace — keep current service, do not run my local project for this service
up.conflict.opt_use_workspace_stop_local: Use workspace — stop my local service and use the one from the workspace merge
up.conflict.opt_local_once: Use my local project (this time only) — keep it running, skip this service in the merge
up.conflict.opt_local_always: Always use my local project — remember this choice and do not ask again
up.conflict.pref_saved_local: Preference saved: use local project for this service
up.conflict.pref_saved_cloned: Preference saved: use cloned service for this service
up.conflict.pref_saved_always_local: Preference saved: always use local project for this service
up.conflict.workspace_already_running: This workspace is already running a different project
up.conflict.merge_explanation: You can merge both configs (volumes and variables from both apply together)
up.conflict.replace_explanation: or use only this project's config (replace). Summary of differences:
up.conflict.how_to_proceed: How do you want to proceed?

up.conflict.opt_replace: [2] Replace: use only this project's config (current deployment will match this project only)
up.conflict.opt_keep: [3] Keep the already running project as-is (do nothing)
up.conflict.opt_merge_remember: [4] Merge and remember for this workspace
up.conflict.opt_replace_remember: [5] Replace and remember for this workspace
up.conflict.opt_keep_remember: [6] Keep the other project and remember for this workspace
up.conflict.opt_always_ask: [7] Always ask me next time (do not remember)
up.conflict.pref_saved_merge: Preference saved: merge with this project for workspace '%s'
up.conflict.pref_saved_replace: Preference saved: use this project (replace) for workspace '%s'
up.conflict.pref_saved_keep: Preference saved: keep project '%s' for workspace '%s'
up.conflict.pref_saved_always_ask: Next time you will be asked again for workspace '%s'
up.host.already_healthy: Service %s is already running and healthy
up.dep.already_running_warning: Dependency '%s' is already running as a project.
up.dep.will_stop_existing: This will stop the existing project and start the new one.
up.dep.replace_prompt: Do you want to replace it? (yes/no/always/never):
up.dep.keeping_existing: Keeping existing project '%s' running. Dependency will use the existing instance.
up.duplicate.already_running: Project '%s' is already running from workspace.
up.duplicate.will_stop_workspace: This will stop the workspace project and start the local one.
up.duplicate.cancelled: Operation cancelled. The workspace project remains running.
up.duplicate.stop_hint: To start the local project, first stop the workspace project with 'raioz down'.
up.duplicate.project_stopped: Project '%s' stopped from workspace. Proceeding with local project...
up.local.keeping_current_skipping: Keeping current service, skipping local project execution
up.local.already_healthy: Project is already running and healthy
up.local.not_running: Project is not running, nothing to stop
up.dep.conflict_prompt: Conflict for '%s': [1] Keep root / [2] Replace / [3] Abort (default: 1):
up.dep.conflict_replaced: Replaced root configuration for '%s' with service config
check.no_state_found: No saved state found. This is normal for new projects.
check.run_up_hint: Run 'raioz up' to create initial state.
error.check_alignment_suggestion: This may indicate a problem with the state file or configuration. Try running 'raioz down' and then 'raioz up' again.
error.check_could_not_determine_project_: Please provide --file or --project flag to specify the project.
error.check_workspace_resolve_suggestion: Check that the project name is correct. Verify workspace directories exist and are accessible.
error.check_load_config_suggestion: Ensure .raioz.json exists and is valid JSON. Use --file flag to specify a different path if needed.
init.wizard_help: This wizard will help you create a basic .raioz.json configuration file.
init.edit_later: You can add services and infrastructure later by editing the file.
init.step_review: 1. Review and edit .raioz.json to add your services and infrastructure
init.step_up: 2. Run 'raioz up' to start your project
init.infra_select: Select infrastructure (comma-separated): 1) PostgreSQL  2) Redis  3) MySQL  4) MongoDB  5) Custom
error.link_check_status: failed to check if service is linked
error.link_path_exists_as_dir: service path already exists as a directory: %s. To create a symlink, you must first remove or move the existing directory
error.link_already_linked_different: service '%s' is already linked to: %s. Use 'raioz link remove %s' first to unlink it
error.clean_all_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status and permissions. Ensure Docker Compose is installed and working.
error.clean_project_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status with 'docker ps'. Verify that Docker Compose is installed and working.
error.clean_images_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status and permissions. Try running 'docker image prune' manually.
error.clean_networks_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status and permissions. Try running 'docker network prune' manually.
error.clean_volumes_suggestion: Check Docker daemon status and permissions. Try running 'docker volume prune' manually.
error.base_dir_suggestion: Check that workspace directories exist and are accessible. Verify file permissions.
cmd.doctor.short: Check system requirements and environment health
cmd.doctor.long:
Verify that all required tools and configurations are in place.

Checks:
- Docker daemon is installed and running
- Docker Compose v2 is available
- Git is installed
- Disk space is sufficient
- Raioz configuration directory exists

Examples:
  raioz doctor
doctor.docker_not_installed: Docker is not installed. Install from https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/
doctor.docker_not_running: Docker is installed but not running. Start the Docker daemon.
doctor.compose_not_installed: Docker Compose v2 not found. Install with: docker compose plugin
doctor.git_not_installed: Git is not installed. Install from https://git-scm.com/
doctor.disk_critical: Critical: only %.1f GB free. Free up disk space.
doctor.disk_low: Low: %.1f GB free. Consider freeing disk space.
doctor.raioz_dir_missing: ~/.raioz not found (will be created on first raioz up)
doctor.result_ok: All checks passed. Your system is ready to use raioz.
doctor.result_warning: Some checks have warnings. Raioz should work but review the warnings above.
doctor.result_error: Some checks failed. Fix the errors above before using raioz.
cmd.dev.short: Promote a dependency to local development
cmd.dev.long:
Switch a dependency from its Docker image to a local path for development.
Use --reset to switch back to the original image.
Use --list to see active dev overrides.
cmd.graph.long:
Display the dependency graph between services and dependencies.
Supports ASCII (default), DOT (Graphviz), and JSON output formats.
cmd.snapshot.create.short: Create a snapshot of project volumes
cmd.tunnel.short: Expose a local service to the internet
cmd.tunnel.long:
Create a public tunnel to a local service using cloudflared or bore.
Useful for webhook testing, mobile development, and sharing with teammates.
cmd.migrate.yaml.long: Convert an existing .raioz.json configuration to the new raioz.yaml format.
cmd.up.flag.exclusive: Stop other running projects before starting this one
up.notify_ready: All services are up and running
cmd.env.short: Show environment variables for a service
cmd.env.long:
Display all environment variables that a service would receive,
including variables from .env files and auto-injected service discovery variables.
env.no_vars: No environment variables found for service '%s'
env.is_dependency: '%s' is a dependency, not a service
env.is_dependency_suggestion: Use raioz env with a service name. Dependencies receive env vars from their env file directly.
cmd.clone.short: Clone a repo and start the project
cmd.clone.flag.no_up: Clone only, do not run raioz up
clone.failed_suggestion: Check the repo URL, your SSH keys, and network connection
clone.no_config: No raioz.yaml found. Run raioz init to generate one.
cmd.dashboard.long:
Opens an interactive terminal dashboard showing all services,
their status, logs, and resource usage in real-time.
Alias: raioz tui
port.conflict.occupied_by_container: Port %d is in use by container '%s'
port.conflict.occupied_by_external: Port %d is in use by an external process (not Docker)
port.conflict.occupied_by_raioz: Port %d is in use by raioz project '%s'
port.conflict.choose_resolution: How would you like to resolve this?
port.conflict.opt_skip: I'll resolve it myself (stop raioz up)
port.conflict.retry_after_resolve: Resolve the conflict and run 'raioz up' again
port.conflict.port_also_in_use: Port %d is also in use, try another
